posts.raw_content:
Companies use illegals like they use H1-B, for cheap labor. They probsbly dont have to extend benefits and they hire on a contract basis or short term with renewals if need be. These people are also paid cinsiderably less than the salary an American citizen wluld command. A citizen would have actual bills to pay - car, home/apartment rental, pissibly a family and all the expenses that go with that. Illegals? Nah they're in SNAP Theyre put up in Section 8 or similar housing. H1-B live like 6 to a 2 bedroom apartment and rotate out their hobs only coming "home" to sleep and whatever. They are usually crammed into an apartment thats only supposed to have like 2 or 3 tennants max living in, they send just about all the money home to India. They dont have the expenses so they dont need to command a bigger salary. Americans with financial obligations lose out.   
But then the left whines about "muh livable wage". They keep demanding a minimum wage go up perpetually as their costs rise. No new work being done to add value to their employment and working for companies who cant afford it given the type of business. If there were no H1-Bs or illegals to hire, companies would have to hire the Americans and youd be able to command a better salary.  When the companies have choices that are cheaper, they will exclude you
